title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: circuitscaper requires Julia (>= 1.9) Circuitscape Omniscape packages. can install everything R: already Julia installed, cs_setup() find automatically can point :
code: # Install circuitscaper (from GitHub during development) # remotes::install_github("matthewkling/circuitscaper")  library(circuitscaper)  # First time only: install Julia and required packages cs_install_julia() cs_setup(julia_home = "/path/to/julia/bin")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: circuitscaper provides four Circuitscape modes one Omniscape mode. ’s choose: Pairwise common starting point. Use discrete sites (habitat patches, populations) want know well-connected . resistance matrix useful distance metric analyses isolation resistance population genetics. One--/ --one variants produce different current maps. One--emphasizes current disperses outward site; --one emphasizes current converges site. Use care spatial pattern connectivity individual nodes, just pairwise resistances. Advanced want full control source ground placement rather using focal nodes. useful modeling directional movement defined source area destination. Omniscape fundamentally different — doesn’t require focal nodes . uses moving window compute omnidirectional connectivity everywhere landscape. Use wall--wall connectivity mapping.

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: common Circuitscape analysis computes effective resistance cumulative current flow pairs focal nodes. can provide focal nodes coordinates (matrix data.frame x/y values) raster integer IDs.
code: library(terra) library(circuitscaper)  # Resistance surface (higher values = harder to traverse) resistance <- rast("path/to/resistance.tif")  # Option 1: Focal nodes as coordinates (simplest) coords <- matrix(c(-120.5, 37.2,                     -119.8, 36.9,                     -121.1, 37.8), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, coords)  # Option 2: Focal nodes as a raster (integer IDs; 0 and NA are not nodes) locations <- rast("path/to/focal_nodes.tif") result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations)  # Current flow map plot(result$current_map)  # Pairwise resistance matrix result$resistance_matrix

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: one--mode, focal node takes turn source others grounded simultaneously. result includes per-node current map focal node plus cumulative map summing across iterations.
code: result <- cs_one_to_all(resistance, locations) plot(result[["cumulative_current"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: --one reverse: focal nodes inject current node takes turn ground.
code: result <- cs_all_to_one(resistance, locations) plot(result[["cumulative_current"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Advanced mode gives full control source ground placement. returns current map voltage map.
code: source_layer <- rast("path/to/sources.tif") ground_layer <- rast("path/to/grounds.tif")  result <- cs_advanced(resistance, source_layer, ground_layer)  # Access individual layers plot(result[["cumulative_current"]]) plot(result[["voltage"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Omniscape computes omnidirectional connectivity using moving window approach. require focal nodes — every cell can serve source.
code: result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100, block_size = 5)  plot(result[["normalized_current"]]) plot(result[["flow_potential"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
code: source_strength <- rast("path/to/habitat_quality.tif")  result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100,                  source_strength = source_strength,                  block_size = 5)  plot(result[["normalized_current"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: large landscapes, enable Julia multithreading. Julia’s thread count fixed startup, set via cs_setup() circuitscaper calls:
code: # Set thread count at the start of your session cs_setup(threads = 4)  result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100,                  block_size = 5,                  parallelize = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: default, intermediate files written temporary directory cleaned . persist output files, use output_dir:
code: result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations,                       output_dir = "my_output_directory")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: CRS preservation: circuitscaper captures CRS input raster reattaches outputs, even though Circuitscape’s ASCII grid format doesn’t carry CRS information. Solver choice: default "cg+amg" solver works well cases. small problems, "cholmod" (direct solver) may faster. Verbose output: Set verbose = TRUE see Circuitscape’s solver progress, useful debugging monitoring long runs.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Matthew Kling. Author, maintainer.

what: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: R interface Circuitscape.jl Omniscape.jl via JuliaCall.

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: Circuitscape Omniscape open-source Julia packages modeling landscape connectivity using circuit theory, developed Brad McRae, Viral Shah, Ranjan Anantharaman, collaborators. Circuitscape treats landscape electrical circuit, raster cell node cells lower resistance allow “current” (representing movement probability gene flow) pass . captures just single best path sites, possible pathways simultaneously, making especially useful identifying corridors pinch points. Omniscape extends applying Circuitscape moving window across landscape, producing wall--wall connectivity maps without requiring predefined focal sites. circuitscaper independent R package (affiliated Circuitscape development team) provides clean, R-native interface tools. Users work entirely R familiar terra::SpatRaster objects Julia handles computation invisibly.

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("matthewkling/circuitscaper")  # First time: install Julia and required packages library(circuitscaper) cs_install_julia()

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
code: library(circuitscaper) library(terra)  # Load resistance surface resistance <- rast("path/to/resistance.tif")  # Focal nodes as coordinates (x, y) coords <- matrix(c(-120.5, 37.2,                     -119.8, 36.9,                     -121.1, 37.8), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)  # Pairwise Circuitscape result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, coords) plot(result$current_map) result$resistance_matrix  # Omniscape moving-window connectivity result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100, block_size = 5) plot(result[["normalized_current"]])

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: R >= 4.0 Julia >= 1.9 (installed automatically via cs_install_julia()) R packages: terra, JuliaCall

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: Circuitscape user guide Omniscape documentation McRae, B.H. (2006). Isolation resistance. Evolution, 60(8), 1551-1561. McRae, B.H. & Beier, P. (2007). Circuit theory predicts gene flow plant animal populations. PNAS, 104(50), 19885-19890. McRae, B.H., Dickson, B.G., Keitt, T.H. & Shah, V.B. (2008). Using circuit theory model connectivity ecology, evolution, conservation. Ecology, 89(10), 2712-2724.

what: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
title: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
text: Provides R-native interface Circuitscape.jl Omniscape.jl Julia packages landscape connectivity modeling using circuit theory. Users work entirely R familiar objects (SpatRaster, file paths) Julia handles computation invisibly. Supports four Circuitscape modes (pairwise, one--, --one, advanced) Omniscape moving-window analysis.

title: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
text: Maintainer: Matthew Kling mattkling@gmail.com

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Solve single circuit user-specified source ground layers.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
code: cs_advanced(   resistance,   source,   ground,   resistance_is = "resistances",   four_neighbors = FALSE,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. source terra::SpatRaster file path. Source current strengths (amps per cell). Cells value 0 NA sources. ground terra::SpatRaster file path. Ground conductances (siemens per cell). Cells value 0 NA grounds. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: cumulative_current Current density cell. voltage Voltage cell. Voltage analogous movement probability decreases distance sources.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Unlike Circuitscape modes, advanced mode iterate focal nodes. Instead, user provides explicit source current ground conductance rasters, single circuit solved. gives full control current injection pattern useful modeling specific scenarios directional movement defined source area destination.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) src <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) src[1, 1] <- 1 gnd <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) gnd[10, 10] <- 1 result <- cs_advanced(res, src, gnd) plot(result) plot(result[["voltage"]]) } # }

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: focal node turn, inject current focal nodes ground single node.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
code: cs_all_to_one(   resistance,   locations,   resistance_is = "resistances",   four_neighbors = FALSE,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. Use resistance_is argument surface represents conductances instead. locations Focal node locations, provided : terra::SpatRaster positive integer IDs identifying node. Cells value 0 NA treated focal nodes. file path raster file (e.g., .tif, .asc). two-column matrix data.frame x/y coordinates. row becomes focal node, auto-assigned IDs 1, 2, 3, ... row order. Coordinates snapped nearest cell resistance raster (must SpatRaster case). See cs_locations(). resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory cleaned automatically. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: cumulative_current Current flow summed across iterations. curmap_N Per-node current map focal node N, N integer node ID locations raster. One layer per focal node.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: --one mode iterates focal node. iteration, focal nodes injected 1 amp current , focal node connected ground. produces current map showing current converges node across landscape. mode useful identifying accessible reachable sites network, emphasizing current flow toward ground node. cumulative map sums across iterations highlights cells important connectivity across full set nodes.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3 result <- cs_all_to_one(res, locs) plot(result) } # }

what: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
title: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
text: Convert set point coordinates focal node raster suitable use cs_pairwise(), cs_one_to_all(), cs_all_to_one(). point snapped nearest cell resistance raster assigned sequential integer ID.

title: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
code: cs_locations(coords, resistance)

title: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
text: coords two-column matrix data.frame x y coordinates. row represents one focal node. IDs assigned sequentially (1, 2, 3, ...) based row order. resistance terra::SpatRaster used template extent, resolution, CRS. output raster match exactly.

title: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
text: terra::SpatRaster 0 background positive integer IDs cells nearest coordinate. can passed directly locations argument cs_* function.

title: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
text: Coordinates snapped nearest raster cell center using terra::cellFromXY(). two points snap cell, error raised. Points fall outside raster extent also produce error. function called internally pass coordinates directly cs_pairwise(), cs_one_to_all(), cs_all_to_one(). Use explicitly want inspect modify focal node raster running analysis.

title: Create a Focal Node Raster from Coordinates — cs_locations
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10))  coords <- matrix(c(-140, 70,                     -100, 30,                      -60, 50), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) locs <- cs_locations(coords, res) plot(locs)  # Equivalent to passing coords directly: result <- cs_pairwise(res, coords) } # }

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: One--mode iterates focal node. iteration, focal node injected 1 amp current remaining focal nodes simultaneously connected ground. produces current map showing current spreads node landscape reach others. mode useful mapping well site connected rest focal node network, emphasizing current dispersal source. cumulative map sums across iterations highlights cells important connectivity across full set nodes.

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3 result <- cs_one_to_all(res, locs) plot(result) } # }

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: named list : current_map terra::SpatRaster single layer: cumulative_current Current flow summed across pairs, indicating relative importance cell movement corridor. resistance_matrix symmetric numeric matrix pairwise effective resistances focal nodes, node IDs row column names.

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: Pairwise mode iterates every unique pair focal nodes. pair, one node injected 1 amp current connected ground. effective resistance pair recorded, resulting current flow accumulated across pairs cumulative current map highlights important movement corridors. common Circuitscape mode typically used quantify connectivity discrete habitat patches populations. resistance matrix can used distance metric analyses isolation resistance.

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3 result <- cs_pairwise(res, locs) plot(result$current_map) result$resistance_matrix } # }

what: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Initialize Julia session load Circuitscape Omniscape Julia packages. called automatically first use cs_* os_* function. Call explicitly control Julia path, number threads, pre-warm session.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
code: cs_setup(julia_home = NULL, threads = 1L, quiet = TRUE, ...)

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: julia_home Character. Path Julia installation directory. NULL (default), JuliaCall searches standard locations. threads Integer. Number Julia threads start. Default 1L. Must set Julia initializes — Julia running, thread count changed without restarting R. Set value greater 1 plan use os_run() parallelize = TRUE. quiet Logical. Suppress Julia startup messages. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed JuliaCall::julia_setup().

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Following pattern established diffeqr, cs_setup() : Install Julia automatically missing (via installJulia = TRUE). Install Circuitscape Omniscape Julia packages missing. Pass ... JuliaCall::julia_setup() users can set JULIA_HOME, rebuild, etc. Julia initialized, stays warm R session. Subsequent calls cs_setup() return immediately.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Julia's thread count fixed startup changed mid-session. need parallelization Omniscape, set threads calling circuitscaper function:
code: cs_setup(threads = 4) os_run(resistance, radius = 50, parallelize = TRUE)

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Circuitscape: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/ Omniscape: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Omniscape.jl/latest/

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cs_setup() cs_setup(threads = 4) cs_setup(julia_home = "/usr/local/julia/bin") } # }

what: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: Performs Omniscape analysis, computing omnidirectional landscape connectivity using moving window approach based circuit theory.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
code: os_run(   resistance,   radius,   source_strength = NULL,   block_size = 1L,   source_threshold = 0,   r_cutoff = Inf,   resistance_is = "resistances",   calc_normalized_current = TRUE,   calc_flow_potential = TRUE,   condition = NULL,   condition_type = NULL,   parallelize = FALSE,   julia_threads = 2L,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. Higher values represent greater resistance movement. radius Numeric. Moving window radius pixels. determines maximum distance connectivity evaluated source pixel. source_strength Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Source strength weights, often derived habitat quality suitability, higher values indicate stronger sources movement. NULL (default), source strength set inverse resistance (.e., non-nodata pixels become sources, weighted conductance). Use r_cutoff exclude high-resistance cells acting sources case. block_size Integer. Aggregation block size source points. Default 1 (aggregation). block_size e.g. 3 coarsens source grid 3x3 blocks, reducing number solves (thus computation time) substantially typically negligible effects results. source_threshold Numeric. Minimum source strength include pixel. Default 0. r_cutoff Numeric. Maximum resistance value cell included source source_strength = NULL. Cells resistance value excluded sources. Default Inf (cutoff). relevant source_strength provided. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". calc_normalized_current Logical. Compute normalized current flow. Default TRUE. calc_flow_potential Logical. Compute flow potential. Default TRUE. condition Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Conditional layer targeted connectivity analysis. condition_type Character. Determines condition layer used. relevant condition provided. See Omniscape documentation options. parallelize Logical. Use Julia multithreading. Default FALSE. Julia's thread count fixed startup. Julia already initialized without enough threads, warning issued. avoid , call cs_setup() threads argument start session. julia_threads Integer. Number Julia threads parallelize = TRUE. Default 2. Ignored Julia already running fewer threads. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print Omniscape output. Default FALSE.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers (depending options): cumulative_current Raw cumulative current flow. Always present. Higher values indicate cells carry current across moving-window iterations. flow_potential Expected current homogeneous resistance (calc_flow_potential = TRUE). Reflects spatial configuration sources independently landscape resistance. normalized_current Cumulative current divided flow potential (calc_normalized_current = TRUE). Values greater 1 indicate cells connectivity higher expected given source geometry; values less 1 indicate relative barriers. typically informative layer identifying corridors pinch points.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: Omniscape documentation: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Omniscape.jl/latest/

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 50, ncols = 50, vals = runif(2500, 1, 10)) result <- os_run(res, radius = 10) plot(result) plot(result[["normalized_current"]]) } # }
